source.title: What Learning Systems do Intelligent Agents Need? Complementary Learning Systems Theory Updated

confidence_rationale: The most directly applicable paper for REE's architectural decisions. Kumaran et al. update CLS theory to address AI systems, broadening the role of hippocampal replay to include goal-dependent weighting of experience statistics -- this is precisely the mechanism REE needs (replay weighted by harm/goal salience, not uniform). They also show recurrent hippocampal activation can support generalization (not just storage), grounding ARC-018's rollout-viability mapping. The paper explicitly bridges neuroscience and deep RL, authored by Google DeepMind researchers who understand both. Very high relevance to SD-004 (structured hippocampal store enabling long-horizon planning) and ARC-007 (indexed path storage with goal-weighted replay).
failure_signatures: The updated CLS theory accepts rapid neocortical learning for schema-consistent information, which complicates the clean separation between hippocampal (fast) and neocortical (slow) stores -- REE's hard module boundaries may be too rigid.

Goal-dependent replay weighting is proposed but the mechanism for communicating goal states to the hippocampal replay selection process is underspecified -- MECH-022's control-plane gate is REE's answer to this gap, but the biological evidence is thin.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Intelligent agents need two learning systems: one that gradually acquires structured knowledge (neocortex) and one that quickly learns specifics of individual experiences (hippocampus). Replay allows goal-dependent weighting of experience statistics. Recurrent hippocampal activation can support generalization beyond simple storage.
mapping.ree_translation: REE's E1/E2/E3 stack and HippocampalModule directly instantiate the two-system architecture. Goal-dependent replay weighting maps onto REE's harm-salience-weighted replay (harmful trajectories replayed more frequently), which is the mechanism by which the residue field accumulates non-uniform density. ARC-007 (paths stored and replayed) and ARC-018 (rollout viability mapping updated by E3 harm/goal error) are both grounded by this paper. SD-004 (action-object map backbone) is the concrete representational choice that enables goal-dependent indexing.
mapping.mapping_caveat: Kumaran et al. do not specify the representational format of hippocampal storage (what gets indexed). REE's choice of action-object coordinates (SD-004) is a design decision beyond what CLS theory specifies -- it requires independent justification from Stachenfeld 2017 and the successor representation literature. The paper also leaves the replay selection mechanism underspecified.
mapping.source_context: Theoretical review bridging neuroscience (rodent + human) and deep learning; authored by Google DeepMind and Stanford

confidence_components.notes: Trends in Cognitive Sciences, highly cited, directly bridges neuroscience and AI design. Mapping fidelity high: paper explicitly addresses AI agent design. Transfer risk low: domain-general architectural argument.
